svg.title: EngineLab development process
svg.text: Given any chess variant → build a framework to create an engine for it
Neutral synthesis; accept if no failure mode raised
Hard cap: 3 rounds max · prevents infinite debate loops · accept if no concrete failure mode raised
Neural nets infeasible given time limit — alpha-beta proven effective pre-ML (Shannon 1950, Knuth 1975)
How to select feature subsets to test?
2^n – 1 subsets grows exponentially · need a strategy that is both feasible and deterministic
Council votes on the 7 best features
Chosen: team directly selects the 7 best features
Council agreement on which 7 features matter most → 127 subsets · fully exhaustive · deterministic · feasible
7 features → 127 subset agents → alpha-beta tournament → marginal analysis → strategy report
Human checkpoint: plan approved before any code written · interfaces locked
Foundation — Person 1, committed to main first
Board · Move · move gen · castling · en passant · check detection · standard variant · stubs for all modules
branch from main → three parallel tracks with zero shared mutable files
CI runs on every push · Area 2 uses mock_play_game() — zero dependency on Area 1 progress
Testers validate against each track while implementation continues in parallel
Area 1 → main first · Area 2 rebases and swaps mock_play_game → play_game (one-line change)
UI wires to real results · all pytest suites pass · human checkpoint before merge
127 agents → 16,002 games · independent pairs run via multiprocessing
per-game seed = tournament_seed + game_index · fully deterministic
Dedicated testers run the full pipeline while engineers fix bugs and add features in parallel
Same seed → identical leaderboard on any run · results are fully reproducible
Leaderboard · marginal contributions · pairwise synergy matrix · strategy report
Cross-variant comparison shows different feature rankings per variant — the core finding
